newsBriefing: Tottenham's relegation anxiety deepened after another collapse, with Crystal Palace punishing a first-half red card and turning a 1-0 deficit into a convincing 3-1 win in north London.

games.headline: Van de Ven red card sparks Spurs collapse as Palace pile on relegation pressure
games.brief: Tottenham had the game exactly where they wanted it after Dominic Solanke's opener, then watched it unravel in minutes when Micky van de Ven was sent off for hauling down Ismaila Sarr in the box. Palace converted the penalty and struck twice more before halftime, turning the match into a survival-night nightmare for Spurs. A spirited 10-man second half could not erase the damage, and another defeat intensified scrutiny on a side now fully in relegation trouble.
bullets: Tottenham led 1-0 through Dominic Solanke in the 34th minute before the match flipped.
Micky van de Ven was shown a straight red for pulling back Ismaila Sarr in the area.
Sarr scored the penalty and then added a second in first-half stoppage time.
Jorgen Strand Larsen also scored before the break as Palace built a 3-1 halftime lead.
Spurs are now 11 Premier League matches without a win, a new club record in the competition.
games.context: The loss leaves 16th-placed Tottenham with relegation pressure rising fast, while Palace banked a high-value away result.

highlights.title: Tottenham Hotspur v. Crystal Palace | PREMIER LEAGUE HIGHLIGHTS | 3/5/2026 | NBC Sports
